title: Equality, Affirmative Action, Caste, NCSC
lead: With the world's most comprehensive ==affirmative action policy== and one of the largest welfare expenditures, India understands the nuances of Equality like none other.

items.title: Fundamental Rights
bullets: ==Art 14==: equality before law & equal protection; anti-arbitrariness
==Art 15(1)–(2)==: no discrimination on religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth
==Art 15(3)–(6)==: special provisions for women & children; SEBC/SC/ST in education; ==EWS via 103rd CA==
==Art 16(1)–(2)==: equal opportunity in public employment
==Art 16(4)/(4A)/(4B)==: reservation for backward classes; ==promotions for SC/ST==; carry-forward of vacancies
==Art 17==: abolition of untouchability · ==Art 18==: abolition of titles
==Arts 23–24==: ban trafficking/forced labour; bar hazardous child labour

items.title: DPSP & Fundamental Duties
bullets: ==Arts 38, 39(a)–(d), 39A, 41–43, 45, 46==: socio-economic equality, equal pay, legal aid, protection of weaker sections
==Art 51A(e)==: renounce practices derogatory to the dignity of women

items.title: Other Constitutional Provisions
bullets: ==Art 335==: SC/ST claims consistent with efficiency
==Arts 330–342==: SC/ST/women representation; identification lists
==Art 342A & 338/338A/338B==: OBC lists; ==NCBC, NCSC, NCST==
==Arts 243D & 243T==: reservation in Panchayats/Municipalities (incl. women)

items.title: Core Statutes & Schemes
bullets: ==SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989==; Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955
RPwD Act 2016; Transgender Persons Act 2019; HIV/AIDS Act 2017
Maternity Benefit (Amendment) 2017; ==POSH Act 2013==; ==RTE 2009== (25% seats)
Forest Rights Act 2006; PESA 1996; Equal Remuneration Act 1976 → ==Code on Wages 2019==
MGNREGA, NFSA, Ayushman Bharat, PM-JANMAN, scholarships

head: Theme
Cases
rows: [[Indra Sawhney (1992)]] (27% OBC valid; ==creamy layer==; ==50% cap==; no promotions) · [[M. Nagaraj (2006)]] & [[Jarnail Singh (2018)]] (promotions framework) · [[Janhit Abhiyan (2022)]] (==EWS upheld==) · [[State of Punjab v. Davinder Singh (2024)]] (==sub-categorisation permitted==)
[[Indian Young Lawyers Assn (Sabarimala) (2018)]] — pending larger-bench review
[[UoI v. Rohith Nathan (2025)]] — parental salary alone can't determine creamy layer · [[Chinthada Anand v. State of AP (2026)]] — converts can't claim SC status

items.title: Education
bullets: [[Champakam Dorairajan (1951)]]: caste reservations unconstitutional → ==1st CA inserted Art 15(4)==
[[T.M.A. Pai (2002)]] & [[P.A. Inamdar (2005)]]: no reservations in private unaided institutions → ==93rd CA inserted Art 15(5)==
[[Janhit Abhiyan (2022)]]: ==103rd CA / 10% EWS upheld==

items.title: Employment
bullets: [[Indra Sawhney (1992)]]: OBC reservation under Art 16(4); ==50% cap==; creamy layer; no promotions
[[M. Nagaraj (2006)]]: promotions allowed with ==quantifiable data== (backwardness, representation, efficiency)
[[Rajeev Kumar Gupta (2016)]]: promotion reservation for ==persons with disabilities==
[[Jarnail Singh (2018)]]: dropped backwardness proof for SC/ST
[[Davinder Singh (2024)]]: ==sub-classification within SC/ST valid==

items.title: Political
bullets: ==Caste-based vote mobilisation== — tickets and portfolios by caste arithmetic (Yadav–Muslim in UP, Dalit-Bahujan BSP, Patidar mobilisation)
Caste-based parties: RJD, DMK, JD(U), AIADMK, PMK, LJP, Apna Dal
Electoral reservation reinforces caste as a ==political resource==

items.title: Economic & Occupational
bullets: ==Safai Karamchari Andolan (2023): ~85% manual scavengers are Dalits==
Caste in entrepreneurship: Agarwals in trade, Nadars in business, Chettiars in finance
==Oxfam India 2022==: upper castes dominate high-paying private jobs

items.title: Social & Cultural
bullets: ==IHDS-II: ~90% marriages intra-caste==; matrimonial caste filters, ‘Caste No Bar’ <10%
Temple priesthood and purity rules still caste-based
Housing segregation; dining taboos persist

items.title: Institutional & Educational
bullets: ==Darshan Solanki case (IIT Bombay, 2023)== → UGC anti-discrimination guidelines
==Rohini Commission (2023)==: 97% OBC benefits captured by 25% sub-castes

items.title: Law, Data & Governance
bullets: ==NCRB 2022: 57,582 crimes against SCs==, 13,535 against STs — 150+ caste crimes daily
==Bihar Caste Survey 2023== reignited proportional-representation debates
EWS and OBC sub-categorisation keep caste salient in policy

items.title: Digital & Media
bullets: Caste-pride social media (#JaatPride, #DalitVoice, #MarathaUnity)
Films — Article 15, Jai Bhim, Maharaj — raise visibility ==and== identity assertion

items.title: Constitutional & Legal
bullets: Arts 14–17; ==PoA Act 1989 (strengthened 2015)==
[[Shakti Vahini (2018)]] — outlawed khap diktats, liberty in marriage
[[Hadiya case (2018)]] — right to choose faith and partner under Art 21
[[Indra Sawhney (1992)]] — creamy layer balances merit and equity

items.title: Educational & Economic Mobility
bullets: ==DoPT (2023): ~50% UPSC entrants from reserved categories==
Ambedkarite student organisations; startups & gig economy reduce occupational rigidity

items.title: Urbanisation & Marriage
bullets: Metro anonymity reduces overt discrimination (Kerala, TN, Maharashtra least segregated)
==Inter-caste marriages 10–12%== (IHDS-II), rising in metros (Mumbai 18%, Bengaluru 16%)
==Dr Ambedkar Scheme==: ₹2.5 lakh incentive for social integration

items.title: Cultural & Global
bullets: Sanskritisation giving way to ==Dalit assertion== — literature festivals, Ambedkarite art
==California (2023): first jurisdiction in the world to ban caste discrimination==

items.title: Lower-Caste Assertion
bullets: Caste identity as ==solidarity and self-respect== against historic disadvantage
Political empowerment: Kanshi Ram, Mayawati, Lalu Prasad, Udit Raj
Movements: ==Bhim Army==, Dalit Panthers 2.0; #DalitHistoryMonth digital activism
Caste census demand — ==Bihar 2023 survey==

items.title: Upper-Caste Anxieties
bullets: ==‘Status anxiety’== from expanding reservation and sub-categorisation
Patel, Maratha, Jat agitations ==demand backward classification==
==EWS quota (2019)== seen as strategy to regain state patronage
‘Merit vs quota’ discourse (AIIMS 2006, IIT Delhi 2023 protests)

blocks.text: Assertion among the oppressed is self-respect; assertion among the privileged is insecurity.
==André Béteille== distinguishes ==status hierarchy (caste)== from ==achievement hierarchy (class)== — modern India is transitioning between both.

items: ==Rural India==: caste and class coincide — landownership mirrors caste hierarchy (Bihar's Rajputs/Yadavs, TN's Thevars/Vellalars). ==Urban India==: class mobility blurs caste markers.
==Post-1991 reforms== expanded ‘achieved status’ — IT, gig and service sectors merit-based; yet ==Oxfam 2022: SC/STs earn 33% less; only 5% Dalits in corporate leadership==.
Rise of a ==‘Dalit middle class’== via education and public jobs — vertical mobility, but prejudice persists.
Class unity across castes: gig-worker strikes (Swiggy, Zomato 2022); but caste networks still mediate recruitment in informal sectors.
==M. N. Srinivas==: ‘Vertical mobility possible, but horizontal barriers remain.’ ==ILO 2023==: caste-linked occupational segregation persists after controlling for education.

items.title: Trends
bullets: Inter-caste: ==10–12% nationally== (IHDS-II), gradual rise in metros
Inter-faith: ==~1%==; urban growth via ==Special Marriage Act, 1954==
Average urban marriage age 28 — longer social exposure increases cross-cultural unions

items.title: Challenges
bullets: ==Khap panchayats== and honour crimes (Haryana, western UP, Rajasthan)
==SMA's 30-day notice== invites harassment — Allahabad HC (2021) urged simplification
==‘Love jihad’ narrative== — anti-conversion laws used against consenting adults
Rental denial, workplace stigma, children's documentation issues

items.title: Responses & Reform
bullets: [[Hadiya (2018)]]: adult autonomy in marriage and religion under ==Art 21==
[[Shakti Vahini (2018)]]: police protection for couples
Kerala's One Stop Centres; Delhi's Love Commandos
Reforms: remove SMA notice clause, fast-track protection, digital registration for privacy

blocks.text: The future of equality in India depends on democratising dignity.

items.title: Mandate & Composition
bullets: Investigates/monitors ==SC safeguards==, inquires into complaints, advises on policy, ==reports to the President== (ATRs laid before Parliament)
==Civil-court powers== while inquiring (summons, documents, affidavits)
Governments must ==consult it== on major policy affecting SCs
Chairperson + Vice-Chairperson + 3 Members, appointed by the President

items.title: Functions & Field Work
bullets: Receives complaints, calls for reports, spot enquiries, recommendations to Union/States
==~47,000 complaints over four years== (2020-21 to 2024)
Field interventions: ==Tirunelveli (Aug 2025)== caste-murder inquiry; ==Karur (Oct 2025)== stampede visit

items.title: Powers — Recommendatory Only
bullets: [[All India Indian Overseas Bank SC/ST Welfare Assn (1996)]]: Art 338(8) gives ==no injunction power==
Delhi HC (2024): NCSC cannot issue directions ==‘in the nature of a civil/criminal court’==
P&H HC (2025): a State SC Commission ==cannot order FIR registration==

items.title: Limitations
bullets: ==Strong constitutional design, weak enforceability==
Heavy caseload, thin capacity; delayed reporting weakens parliamentary control
Dependence on State machinery; needs to widen from atrocity to ==socio-economic justice==

items.label: crimes against SCs, NCRB 2023 (↑0.4% over 2022); rate 28.7/lakh — UP tops count, MP/Rajasthan in rate
complaints filed with NCSC since 2020 (RTI) — atrocities and land disputes lead

items: ==Reforms==: time-bound ATRs (e.g. 90 days); shared ‘backbone’ across equality bodies (NCSC/NCST/NCBC/NCW/NHRC) — unified case-tracking, forensic panel, victim-services fund
Limited ==binding interim relief powers== (safety directions, ex-gratia) subject to appellate review
==Data pipes with NCRB==: public dashboards linking complaint → FIR → charge-sheet → trial → relief
Fill vacancies; more state benches/mobile sittings; MoUs with Legal Services Authorities; ==independent investigation wing==

head: Dimension
India
United States
United Kingdom
France
rows: 14th Amendment Equal Protection; 5th Amendment due process; statutes
Formal equality; quotas disfavoured; parity measures
Race-conscious admissions struck down — [[SFFA v. Harvard/UNC (2023)]]; quotas barred since [[Bakke (1978)]]
Reasonable classification + anti-arbitrariness; creamy layer; ==50% cap (EWS exception)==
Proportionality via HRA; statutory justification tests
Proportionality; Conseil constitutionnel polices equality & laïcité
SC/HC writs; NCSC/NCST/NCBC; special courts (PoA Act)
Conseil constitutionnel/Conseil d'État; Défenseur des droits
